title: [Bug]: Payload normalization silently accepts duplicate JSON keys
summary: Payload normalization rejects non-standard constants but still accepts duplicate JSON object keys because the shared JSON parser uses default duplicate-key behavior.
observed: A payload such as {"kind":"first","kind":"second"} can be preserved as JSON with only the final value retained.
expected: Duplicate keys should be treated as ambiguous JSON. json_only mode should raise SerializationError, while json_or_envelope mode should preserve the original text envelope instead of silently dropping information.
reproduction: Run the focused regression test that normalizes a payload with duplicate JSON keys in json_only and json_or_envelope modes.
impact: The defect can affect all JSON-capable sinks because duplicate-key payloads may be persisted after losing one of the original values.
delivery_semantics: Commit-then-ACK behavior must not change. The fix only changes how ambiguous JSON is classified before sink storage.
security: The fix must not log raw payload bodies. Error messages may include the subject but not payload content.
acceptance: A focused regression test proves duplicate payload JSON keys are treated as ambiguous.
json_or_envelope mode preserves the original text in the standard payload envelope.
Existing Oracle and file sink mapping tests still pass.
tests: Run the focused regression test, payload normalization tests, envelope tests, Oracle mapping tests, file mapping tests, and the full local check script.
documentation: Update CHANGELOG.md and payload documentation to mention duplicate-key handling.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published. Include the release tag, regression evidence, verification evidence, and any remaining limitations.
